$comment: Shared, tracked project settings for schnapp-os. Local-only overrides go in settings.local.json. autoMemoryDirectory points the harness auto-memory at the git-tracked repo memory/ lane so memory commits and syncs across machines/surfaces (PLAN.md Part 5; see memory/README.md); the same value is set at USER scope in ~/.claude/settings.json so the lane is active everywhere — plugins can't set this key (handoff 023). HOOKS (scripts in plugins/core/hooks/, canonical procedures in memory/README.md): all three are now wired HERE against live ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} paths — SessionStart freshness gate (session-start-gate.sh), Stop push-gate (session-stop-push-gate.sh), SessionEnd backup (session-end-backup.sh). This replaces the prior split where SessionStart+Stop were delivered globally by the claude-kit-core plugin: per decision 0011 #2 (plainer repo, drop plugin-packaging reliance) and because plugin snapshot delivery was unreliable (installed_plugins.json pinned old commits / referenced cleaned cache paths, so a stale claude-kit-era gate fired), hooks read the live repo file every session — no snapshot drift. The plugin hooks.json now declares no hooks. These hooks are scoped to schnapp-os (the OS repo); cross-surface freshness for hookless surfaces is the remote-MCP layer's job. Project hooks honored after the per-machine workspace-trust dialog; hooks load at session start (restart to pick up changes).
